messages.content:
(explain in one sentence) Calculate quantities of a
substance or its relative
number of particles using
dimensional analysis and the
mole concept.
Dimensional analysis and the mole concept can be used to calculate the quantity of a substance or its relative number of particles by converting between units of mass, number of particles, and moles.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the quantitative
relationship between the
mass spectrum of an element
and the masses of the
element’s isotopes.

The mass spectrum of an element displays the relative abundance and masses of its isotopes, allowing for the determination of the average atomic mass of the element based on the contributions of each isotope.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the quantitative
relationship between the
elemental composition by
mass and the empirical
formula of a pure substance.

The elemental composition by mass, determined through analytical techniques such as combustion analysis, can be used to derive the empirical formula of a pure substance by determining the relative ratios of elements present in the compound.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the quantitative
relationship between the
elemental composition by
mass and the composition of
substances in a mixture.
The elemental composition by mass can be used to calculate the composition of substances in a mixture by determining the relative amounts of different elements present in the mixture.

(explain in one sentence)  Represent the electron
configuration of an element
or ions of an element using
the Aufbau principle.
The electron configuration of an element or ions of an element can be represented using the Aufbau principle by filling electron orbitals in order of increasing energy levels.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the relationship
between the photoelectron
spectrum of an atom or
ion and:
a. The electron configuration
of the species.
b. The interactions
between the electrons
and the nucleus.

The photoelectron spectrum of an atom or ion can provide information about their electron configuration by showing the energy levels of ejected electrons and interactions between the electrons and the nucleus based on the binding energies observed.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the relationship
between trends in atomic
properties of elements and
electronic structure
and periodicity.

The trends in atomic properties of elements, such as atomic radius, ionization energy, and electron affinity, are determined by the electronic structure and periodicity, with patterns arising from the arrangement of electrons in the periodic table.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the relationship
between trends in the
reactivity of elements
and periodicity

The reactivity of elements follows periodic trends in the periodic table, with elements in the same group exhibiting similar reactivity due to their similar electron configurations and tendencies to lose, gain, or share electrons.

(explain in one sentence)  Explain the relationship
between the type of bonding
and the properties of the
elements participating in
the bond.

The type of bonding, whether it is ionic, covalent, or metallic, influences the properties of the elements participating in the bond, resulting in differences in characteristics such as conductivity, melting point, and hardness.

(explain in one sentence) Represent the relationship
between potential energy
and distance between
atoms, based on factors that
influence the interaction
strength.

The relationship between potential energy and distance between atoms is influenced by factors such as the type of bonding (ionic, covalent, or metallic) and intermolecular forces, which determine the strength of interactions between the atoms.

(explain in one sentence) Represent an ionic solid with
a particulate model that is
consistent with Coulomb’s
law and the properties of the
constituent ions.

An ionic solid can be represented with a particulate model that reflects Coulomb's law, as the strong electrostatic attraction between positively and negatively charged ions results in a regular, three-dimensional lattice structure with high melting points and brittle properties.

(explain in one sentence)  Represent a metallic
solid and/or alloy using a
model to show essential
characteristics of the
structure and interactions
present in the substance.

A metallic solid and/or alloy can be represented using a model that showcases a closely packed arrangement of metal atoms held together by delocalized electrons, which results in high electrical and thermal conductivity, malleability, and ductility.

(explain in one sentence)  Represent a molecule with a
Lewis diagram.
A Lewis diagram represents a molecule by showing the bonding between atoms using dots to represent valence electrons and lines to represent covalent bonds.

(explain in one sentence) Represent a molecule with a
Lewis diagram that accounts
for resonance between
equivalent structures or that
uses formal charge to select
between nonequivalent
structures.

A Lewis diagram representing a molecule may include resonance structures that show the delocalization of electrons or formal charges to demonstrate different arrangements of the same atoms bonded together.

(explain in one sentence)  Based on the relationship
between Lewis diagrams,
VSEPR theory, bond orders,
and bond polarities:
a. Explain structural
properties of molecules.
b. Explain electron properties
of molecules.

The relationship between Lewis diagrams, VSEPR theory, bond orders, and bond polarities explains the structural properties of molecules by predicting their molecular shape and arrangement of atoms, as well as electron properties by determining the distribution of electrons between atoms and the polarity of bonds.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the relationship
between the chemical
structures of molecules and
the relative strength of their
intermolecular forces when:
a. The molecules are of the
same chemical species.
b. The molecules are of two
different chemical species.

The chemical structures of molecules determine the relative strength of their intermolecular forces, with molecules of the same species having stronger forces when they exhibit hydrogen bonding or dipole-dipole interactions compared to molecules of different species that only show dispersion forces.

(explain in one sentence) Explain the relationship
among the macroscopic
properties of a substance,
the particulate-level structure
of the substance, and the
interactions between these
particles

The macroscopic properties of a substance, such as its physical state, density, and thermal conductivity, are determined by the particulate-level structure and interactions between particles, including bonding forces and arrangement of atoms or molecules.
